name: Client Portal
professionalExpectations: A client portal must provide secure, clear access to project status, documents, requests, messages, and approvals.
Client identity, permissions, file storage, messaging, and production data access are gated until approved.
The first version should prove the portal model with local records and non-live views.
Trust depends on privacy, auditability, clear status, and no accidental exposure.
minimumQualityBar: Client, project, permission, document, request, and message concepts are modeled before live identity exists.
No client data, customer data, production data, credentials, live messaging, file upload, or deployment exists without approval.
Access assumptions, data classification, retention, and rollback are documented.
Read-only and write-capable surfaces are clearly separated.
commonModules: Client dashboard, project status, milestones, documents, requests, approvals, messages, and notifications plan.
Permission model, role placeholders, data classification map, and audit trail.
File and document handling plan with retention, redaction, and backup requirements.
Future connector gates for identity, storage, email, billing, CRM, and support tools.
uxExpectations: Portal views should be simple, trustworthy, and focused on the client's next action.
Status, due dates, approvals, and missing information should be visible immediately.
Documents and messages should be organized by project and topic.
Security and privacy states should be clear without overwhelming the client.

components: Static or local portal prototype from repository records
Identity provider only after approved access model
Existing Postgres only after approved client-data handling
Netlify only after approved staging or production deployment
recommendedStack.notes: Start with portal structure and read-only state. Do not collect or expose client data until access and data rules are approved.

phases.name: Portal Model
goals: Define client, project, document, request, approval, and message concepts.
Map access, data classification, and approval gates.

phases.name: Read-Only Portal
goals: Build local dashboard, project status, and document index views without live data.
Validate navigation, empty states, and privacy labels.

phases.name: Approved Client Access
goals: Add identity, storage, messaging, and notifications only after approval and rollback planning.
Record audit evidence for access and data handling.

knownPitfalls: Adding login and file upload before permissions and data classification are settled.
Exposing internal project data that should not be client-visible.
Overbuilding custom messaging before the portal's core status flow is useful.
approvalGates: Owner approval is required before identity, client data, customer data, production data, file storage, email, messaging, deployment, domain or DNS changes.
Owner approval is required before credentials, paid tools, billing, CRM, support, or external notification integrations.
Owner approval is required before adding write access for clients.
Owner approval is required before migrating any protected project into a portal.
qualityChecklist: Portal entities, visibility, permissions, and client-facing status are clear.
Read-only and write-capable features are separated.
Data classification, retention, backup, audit, and rollback are documented.
No live identity, messaging, file, deployment, or client-data path exists without approval.
whatToBuildFirst: Portal information architecture and permission model.
Read-only project status and document index views.
Data classification and future identity gate checklist.
whatNotToOverbuildEarly: Client login, file upload, live messaging, or notifications before approval.
Complex billing, support, or CRM integrations.
Granular permission matrix before roles are confirmed.
Production storage or deployment before protected data rules are ready.
stopConditions: Stop before client data, customer data, production data, credentials, live identity, file storage, messaging, deployment, domain, DNS, or paid services.
Stop before granting write access or external notifications.
Stop before modifying Lead Gen production or AI Receptionist repo records.
notes: Archetype knowledge record only. It does not create client access, file storage, messaging, or deployment.
